description: Fix Windows-breaking npm spawn calls in CLI and misleading port-unchanged message

userStories.title: Use platformExec('npm') for all npm spawn calls in plugin.ts
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want CLI plugin commands to work so that I can search for, install, and inspect plugins
acceptanceCriteria: All spawn calls in plugin.ts that invoke 'npm' use platformExec('npm') instead of bare 'npm'
Specifically: line 92 (packageExistsOnNpmAsync), line 120 (warnIfNotPlugin), line 282 (fetchPackageInfo), line 348 (npmSearchPlugins) all use platformExec('npm')
Existing calls that already use platformExec('npm') at lines 162, 236, 244, 518, 519 remain unchanged
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts

The file already imports platformExec from @opentabs-dev/shared (line 25) and uses it correctly in several places (lines 162, 236, 244, 518, 519). But four spawn calls use bare 'npm' instead:

- Line 92: `spawnProcessAsync('npm', ['view', pkg, 'version'])` — in packageExistsOnNpmAsync
- Line 120: `spawnProcessAsync('npm', ['root', '-g'])` — in warnIfNotPlugin
- Line 282: `spawnProcessSync('npm', ['view', pkg, ...])` — in fetchPackageInfo
- Line 348: `spawnProcessSync('npm', ['search', searchTerm, '--json'])` — in npmSearchPlugins

On Windows, 'npm' is distributed as npm.cmd. Node's spawn() does not search PATHEXT, so bare 'npm' fails with ENOENT. platformExec() handles this by returning 'npm.cmd' on Windows.

Fix: Replace 'npm' with platformExec('npm') in all four calls. Do NOT change the calls that already use platformExec('npm').

userStories.title: Use platformExec('npm') for npm spawn calls in update.ts
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want the opentabs update command to work so that I can update the CLI to the latest version
acceptanceCriteria: Line 34 in update.ts uses platformExec('npm') instead of bare 'npm' in getLatestVersion()
Line 58 in update.ts uses platformExec('npm') instead of bare 'npm' in performUpdate()
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/update.ts

Two spawnSync calls use bare 'npm':
- Line 34: `spawnSync('npm', ['view', CLI_PACKAGE_NAME, 'version'], ...)` — in getLatestVersion()
- Line 58: `spawnSync('npm', ['install', '-g', target], ...)` — in performUpdate()

Fix: Add `import { platformExec } from '@opentabs-dev/shared';` and replace 'npm' with platformExec('npm') in both calls.

IMPORTANT: The update.ts file uses spawnSync directly from 'node:child_process', not the spawnProcessSync wrapper from plugin.ts. The fix is the same pattern — replace the first arg 'npm' with platformExec('npm').

userStories.title: Fix misleading restart message when port is unchanged in config.ts
userStories.description: As a user, I want accurate feedback when setting the port so that I don't unnecessarily restart the server
acceptanceCriteria: When opentabs config set port <N> is called where N equals the current configured port, no 'restart' message is printed
When the port actually changes, the existing restart message behavior is preserved
